new_value:
{"description":"The opentelemetry-otlp v0.31 (latest) OTLP/HTTP export never actually sends metrics to the collector. Tested extensively with otel-collector-contrib:

- reqwest-blocking-client without rt-tokio: PeriodicReader thread runs but HttpClient trait is async, so export silently fails. force_flush() returns InternalFailure.
- reqwest-blocking-client with rt-tokio: PeriodicReader's std::thread panics 'no reactor running' since thread doesn't inherit tokio context.
- reqwest-client (async) with rt-tokio: Same thread-context issue.
- experimental_metrics_periodicreader_with_async_runtime + rt-tokio + reqwest-client: PeriodicReader spawns tokio task, no panics, force_flush() returns Ok, but zero TCP connections to collector and zero metrics received.

Rust 1.94.0, OTel SDK 0.31.0 are both latest. This is an architectural issue in the SDK, not a version lag. Fix options: (1) switch to grpc-tonic transport, (2) manually POST OTLP payloads with reqwest, (3) wait for upstream fix (see open-telemetry/opentelemetry-rust#3125)."}
old_value: {"id":"lox-bp8","title":"OTel OTLP export broken: PeriodicReader + reqwest-blocking-client incompatible","description":"The opentelemetry-otlp v0.31 reqwest-blocking-client feature doesn't work with the PeriodicReader. Without rt-tokio, force_flush() always returns InternalFailure. With rt-tokio, the PeriodicReader's background thread panics with 'no reactor running'. The reqwest-client (async) has the same thread-context issue. Tested end-to-end with otel-collector — metrics never arrive at the collector. Options: (1) use hyper-client instead, (2) manually construct OTLP payload and POST with reqwest, (3) wait for SDK fix.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"c.neumueller@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:52:45Z","created_by":"Christoph Neumüller","updated_at":"2026-03-14T21:52:45Z"}

new_value: Fixed: switched to async PeriodicReader + reqwest-client + explicit /v1/metrics path + numeric prefix parsing
